fix_decisions.claim_text: the added comment's 'sandbox blocks an out-of-workspace read' claim is a documented_falsehood / the fix is incomplete because 3 sibling /etc/hosts controls are unchanged and broken
fix_decisions.evidence: REFUTED (over-grade calibration gate + verify-before-implementing). Verified against scripts/schedulewakeup-probe-verdict.py collect() (lines 110-124): every tool_use node is recorded independently of permission_denials, and control_before = '/etc/hosts' in tooluse_text (line 140) keys on tool_use PRESENCE. A sandbox-denied command is recorded in BOTH the tool_use stream (the model attempted it) AND permission_denials (the refusal), so 'the sandbox blocks the read' (permission_denials) and 'the control ran' (tool_use present) are SIMULTANEOUSLY TRUE — no contradiction. The siblings' verdict logic is denial-agnostic (schedulewakeup keys on tool_use presence; execfile-shape-probe keys on the execution-file shape; hook-probe keys on the Stop-hook breadcrumb), so they are genuinely unaffected by the #477 denied-first DENIED bug and correctly left unchanged (AC3 scope: only matcher-probe.yml). Promoting this would violate AC3 and be factually wrong. Both requesting-code-review passes independently traced this correctly. Iteration 2 applied a clarity note (the legitimate kernel both requesting-code-review agents recommended) preempting the misreading; the re-shadow's 4 agents all verified the comment accurate against source and returned clean.

fix_decisions.claim_text: README.md marker is collision-prone; a denied entry mentioning it could flip the positive control to a false DENIED
fix_decisions.evidence: over-grade shape 3 (single-source from silent-failure-hunter, no cross-agent corroboration; the two requesting-code-review passes explicitly assessed the same observation as non-blocking / 'never produces a false DENIED'). Same finding as iter-1 SF-1. The probe prompt instructs 'attempt only the 15/7 listed shapes verbatim, add no other command', so no other recorded call references README.md in a compliant run; a false DENIED would require the model to spontaneously run an unlisted denied command carrying 'README.md', which the prompt forbids. Issue #477 explicitly blessed README.md as an acceptable unique marker. The proposed nonce-sidecar fix carries its own regression risk (a model normalizing away an inert trailing '# probeNNctl' comment would make the healthy-run control read a systematic false UNATTEMPTED). Calibrated to Suggestion; parked advisory; not auto-fixed.
